title: dalea frutescens var. laxa
intro: Dalea frutescens var. laxa, also known as prairie rose or smooth skippers-rose, is a perennial legume native to North America. The plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal properties, due to its high content of flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenes. The health benefits of dalea frutescens var. laxa include reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and supporting immune system function. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat various ailments, including digestive issues and skin problems.
